items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Identity.Broker` **Service directory:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/` **Triggered by:** commit 94caeed59e19716c051d68a14fc323c297ab84cc (version increment after release of Azure.Identity.Broker 1.6.0) by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed Azure.Identity.Broker 1.6.0 was released on 2026-04-14 with a critical dependency change: the package now depends on `Azure.Core` directly (not `Azure.Identity`), as the Azure.Identity types moved to Azure.Core in the 1.53.0 type consolidation. Additionally, several features added across versions 1.3.0–1.6.0 remain undocumented in the README. ## Gaps Found - **Outdated Prerequisites section**: Still lists `Azure.Identity` as a prerequisite, but since 1.6.0 the package depends on `Azure.Core` directly. The README should also call out the critical compatibility requirement: version 1.6.0+ is required when using `Azure.Core` 1.53.0+ (or `Azure.Identity` 1.21.0+), with a link to the [migration guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md#azureidentitybroker-compatibility). - **Missing "Examples" section**: The README has no "Examples" section, which is required by the standard README template. There are no sample test files under `tests/Samples/` either. Concrete code examples for creating `InteractiveBrowserCredential` with a parent window handle (HWND) are needed. - **Cross-platform support not documented**: Version 1.3.0 (stable) added macOS and Linux/WSL broker support, but the README only mentions Windows (WAM) implicitly. Platform requirements and limitations should be documented. - **DI/Configuration integration undocumented**: Version 1.4.0 added experimental `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration` and `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection` integration (with link to configuration docs), but the README doesn't mention it. - **JSON schema / appsettings.json IntelliSense undocumented**: Version 1.5.0 added a JSON schema segment enabling IntelliSense and validation for credential configuration in `appsettings.json`, but the README doesn't mention it. - **No `samples/` directory**: Unlike comparable packages, there are no standalone sample files for this package.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/README.md`: 1. **Update Prerequisites section** — replace the existing `Azure.Identity` prerequisite entry: ```markdown ### Prerequisites * An [Azure subscription][azure_sub]. * The [Azure.Core][azure_core] package (1.53.0 or later, included transitively via `Azure.Identity` 1.21.0+). > **Important:** Azure.Identity.Broker 1.6.0 or later is required when your application references `Azure.Core` 1.53.0+ or `Azure.Identity` 1.21.0+. Earlier versions of this package are incompatible with the type consolidation introduced in those releases. See the [migration guide][broker_migration] for details. ``` Update the link references at the bottom of the README to add: ```markdown [azure_core]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md [broker_migration]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md#azureidentitybroker-compatibility ``` 2. **Add "Examples" section** — insert between the "Key concepts" and "Troubleshooting" sections: ```markdown ## Examples ### Authenticate with InteractiveBrowserCredential using a broker ```C# Snippet:Identity_Broker_InteractiveBrowser ``` ``` The snippet content will be filled in by `Update-Snippets.ps1` after writing the sample test file in Step 2. 3. **Add cross-platform support note** in the "Key concepts" section: ```markdown ### Platform support The authentication broker is supported on: - **Windows**: Windows Authentication Manager (WAM) via the built-in broker. - **macOS**: Microsoft broker (added in 1.3.0). - **Linux and WSL**: Microsoft broker (added in 1.3.0). Prerequisites and setup instructions are described in [Enable SSO in native Linux apps using MSAL.NET]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` 4. **Add DI/Configuration integration note** in the "Key concepts" section: ```markdown ### Configuration and dependency injection This package supports optional integration with `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration` and `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection`. For details, see the [Configuration and Dependency Injection](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) documentation. A JSON schema is included in the NuGet package to provide IntelliSense and validation for credential configuration in `appsettings.json`. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **Create** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/tests/Samples/Sample1_InteractiveBroker.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.Identity.Broker; using Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Identity.Broker.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_InteractiveBroker { [Test] [Ignore("This test is a live test that requires user interaction.")] public void InteractiveBrowserWithBroker() { #region Snippet:Identity_Broker_InteractiveBrowser // Obtain the parent window handle for your application. // On a WinForms or WPF application, use the handle of the main form/window. // The value IntPtr.Zero instructs the broker to select an appropriate parent window automatically. IntPtr parentWindowHandle = IntPtr.Zero; var credential = new InteractiveBrowserCredential( new InteractiveBrowserCredentialBrokerOptions(parentWindowHandle)); var client = new SecretClient(new Uri("(myvault.vault.azure.net/redacted), credential); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("This test is a live test that requires user interaction.")] public void InteractiveBrowserWithBrokerUseDefaultAccount() { #region Snippet:Identity_Broker_UseDefaultBrokerAccount // Set UseDefaultBrokerAccount to true to silently use the currently signed-in // operating system account, bypassing the interactive sign-in prompt. IntPtr parentWindowHandle = IntPtr.Zero; var options = new InteractiveBrowserCredentialBrokerOptions(parentWindowHandle) { UseDefaultBrokerAccount = true }; var credential = new InteractiveBrowserCredential(options); #endregion } } } ``` - In the README, insert the following placeholder in the new "Examples" section for the first snippet: ````markdown ```C# Snippet:Identity_Broker_InteractiveBrowser ``` ```` And a second example for default account: ````markdown ### Authenticate with the signed-in OS account (silent) ```C# Snippet:Identity_Broker_UseDefaultBrokerAccount ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/README.md` currently has these sections: | # | Section | Status | |---|---------|--------| | 1 | Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate) | ⚠️ Prerequisites outdated | | 2 | Key concepts | ⚠️ Missing platform support and DI/config notes | | 3 | Examples | ❌ Missing entirely | | 4 | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | 5 | Next steps | ✅ Present | | 6 | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` — this reads `#region Snippet:` tags from test files and injects the code into README.md placeholder blocks. Check the git diff after running to confirm README was updated correctly. 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 identity` — regenerates public API listing files. Required if any public types or members were added or changed. 5. `dotnet format sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/` — ensures consistent code formatting.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above.
items.title: [Identity] Docs: Azure.Identity.Broker README outdated after 1.6.0 release
